feature_name: Dual operation modes: Root Complex (RC) and End Point (EP)
description: The controller supports dual mode operation as either a Root Complex or an End Point. Default mode is selected by PCIESSMODE[1:0] bootstraps with software override possible. As an EP it can operate as legacy or native PCIe endpoint. Mode selection affects configuration header type and feature availability. Link training is software-enabled after mode configuration.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; Section 1.4.1 PCI Express Core Module; Section 2.11 Operations
dependencies: Bootstraps PCIESSMODE[1:0]; CMD_STATUS.LTSSM_EN
Remarks: Choose mode early in initialization; verify header type (Type0 for EP, Type1 for RC).

feature_name: Single link up to x2 with dynamic width, auto lane reversal and polarity inversion
description: A single bidirectional link is provided with maximum width of two lanes (x2). The core supports dynamic-width conversion and automatic lane reversal. Receive polarity inversion is supported to ease board routing. Ultra-low TX/RX latency is targeted. Link width is negotiated at training and exposed in status registers.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; LINK_CAP[9:4] (3.7.5); DEBUG1[0] lane reversal (3.9.12)
dependencies: SerDes lanes configured; PL_LINK_CTRL.LNK_MODE; LTSSM training complete
Remarks: Design PCB for differential pairs; allow auto lane reversal/polarity where routing requires.

feature_name: Gen1/Gen2 line rates and Gen2 directed speed change
description: Per-lane raw speeds of 2.5 and 5.0 Gbps are supported. The PHY keeps PLL output at 2.5 GHz for both speeds and converts data width when switching to Gen2. Software uses PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD to initiate Gen2 speed change after Gen1 link-up. De-emphasis and TX swing levels are configurable to meet channel compliance. Common-clock operation should be indicated via LINK_STAT_CTRL.COMMON_CLK_CFG when used.
evidence: Section 2.3.3 Reference Clock Multiplication; PL_GEN2 (3.9.13); LINK_STAT_CTRL (3.7.6)
dependencies: SerDes PLL locked (PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK); PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD; LINK_STAT_CTRL.COMMON_CLK_CFG
Remarks: Verify signal integrity and de-emphasis settings for the channel; only trigger Gen2 after stable Gen1 link.

feature_name: Address Space 0/1 mapping and configuration windows
description: The module exposes two internal address spaces: Address Space 0 (4x4KB regions) for application registers, local/remote configuration, and remote IO; and Address Space 1 for data transfers. BAR0 always maps Application Registers, enabling RC access to EP control. Remote configuration and IO windows are memory-mapped with caution to avoid accesses when link is down.
evidence: Section 2.8 PCIe Address Spaces; Figure 2-5; Section 2.8.1.1 (Caution)
dependencies: BAR0 decode; CFG windows; Link up (DEBUG0.LTSSM_STATE==0x11)
Remarks: Mark windows as non-cacheable and use single 32-bit accesses as required.

feature_name: Outbound address translation (32 regions)
description: Outbound TLPs can map internal addresses to PCIe addresses using 32 equally-sized regions. Region size is 1/2/4/8 MB set by OB_SIZE and each region’s base is set via OB_OFFSET_INDEXn/OB_OFFSETn_HI. Software enables translation with CMD_STATUS.OB_XLT_EN and programs regions before traffic. Transactions must not cross translation boundaries.
dependencies: CMD_STATUS.OB_XLT_EN=1; Program OB_* registers; Proper region size selection
Remarks: Partition address map so bursts fit inside regions; avoid boundary crossing for large writes.

feature_name: Inbound address translation (4 regions) and BAR association
description: Inbound requests are accepted by BAR decoding and mapped to device memory via four translation regions. Each region matches a BAR via IB_BARn and maps PCIe start to internal offset using IB_STARTn_{LO,HI} and IB_OFFSETn. BAR0 is reserved to application registers and not remappable. A BAR1 shortcut allows using BAR1 as start if IB_START is left zero.
evidence: Section 2.7.2; IB_* registers (3.1.82–3.1.97); BAR0 exception (2.7.2.2); Using BAR1 value (2.7.2.3)
dependencies: CMD_STATUS.IB_XLT_EN=1; Proper BAR programming; IB_* region setup
Remarks: Group non-contiguous host windows with ascending start addresses; ensure 4KB boundary rules are respected.

feature_name: BAR Mask registers and DBI_CS2 access
description: EP and RC may preconfigure BAR size/type via write-only BAR_MASK registers overlaid on BARs. Software must set CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2 before programming masks and clear it afterward, verifying write completion with a readback. BAR0 mask is restricted; BAR0 always targets application registers.
evidence: Section 2.7.3.1 BAR Mask Registers; CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2 (3.1.3); BARx_MASK tables (3.3, 3.4)
dependencies: CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2 handshake; BAR enabled; Link not required
Remarks: Use mask testing pattern to validate sizing; never modify BAR masks from serial link side.

feature_name: DMA and data movement via master/slave VBUSM ports
description: The PCIESS has a 128-bit slave and a 128-bit master VBUSM interface. External DMA (e.g., EDMA) can move data to/from PCIe via the slave port, while the master port services inbound requests from PCIe to internal memory. Example EDMA setups are provided for read/write with payload chunking. Sustained bandwidth targets about 85% of protocol throughput per lane and speed.
dependencies: Configured address translation; EDMA setup; Adequate interconnect bandwidth
Remarks: Align EDMA aCnt/bCnt to max payload and observe 128B/120B alignment rules.

feature_name: Endian modes and swizzle for inbound/outbound data
description: Although PCIe link is little-endian, the subsystem supports big-endian systems using programmable byte swapping. ENDIAN[1:0] selects swap granularity for both inbound and outbound paths. Tables define exact lane mapping per mode to preserve data semantics. Byte enables are swapped consistently with data.
dependencies: System endianness configuration; Program ENDIAN before data traffic
Remarks: Choose mode A/B/C/D per memory width; validate with pattern tests across 64-bit words.

feature_name: Legacy INTx: generation (EP) and reception (RC)
description: As an EP, legacy INTA–INTD can be generated via EP_IRQ_SET/CLR and enabled through LEGACY_X IRQ enable registers. As an RC, INTx messages are received and latched with status/EOI flow. Only one INTx can be pending for an EP function until deassert is sent. Software must account for message delivery race conditions.
dependencies: Enable LEGACY_X IRQ; Proper EOI sequencing; Link up
Remarks: Probe downstream devices to identify source before EOI; ensure deassert processed to avoid retrigger.

feature_name: MSI: generation and reception with vectorized mapping
description: MSI is supported in both RC and EP roles with up to 32 vectors. EP generates MSI by writing the programmed MSI address/data or by targeting RC’s MSI_IRQ register with a vector value. MSI blocks include capability, address, data, and per-vector enable/status groups. Reception sets MSIn_IRQ_STATUS and is cleared by W1C and IRQ_EOI writes.
evidence: Section 2.14.2.2; 2.14.5.2; MSI_CAP/LOW32/UP32/DATA (3.6.2–3.6.5); MSI_IRQ and MSI status blocks (3.1.16, 3.1.24–3.1.55)
dependencies: MSI_CAP.MSI_EN=1; Program MSI LOW/UP32 & DATA; Enable MSIn IRQ
Remarks: Allocate vectors during enumeration; don’t assume ordering between data writes and MSI due to relaxed ordering.

feature_name: Interrupt groups: MSI, Legacy, Error, PM/Reset mapping to 14 events
description: The PCIESS exposes 14 interrupt lines/events to the SoC controller. These cover grouped MSI vectors, legacy INTx (RC-only), aggregated error sources (system, fatal, non-fatal, correctable, AXI), and power/reset events. Software enables, services, and clears via dedicated STATUS/ENABLE/EOI registers.
evidence: Table 2-10 PCIESS Interrupt Events; ERR_IRQ_* (3.1.72–3.1.75); PMRST_* (3.1.76–3.1.79); IRQ_EOI (3.1.15)
dependencies: Enable bits per group; Proper W1C and EOI usage; Link/event source ready
Remarks: Map events to CPU IRQs carefully; clear status before EOI to avoid spurious retriggers.

feature_name: Advanced Error Reporting (AER) and root error handling
description: The controller implements PCIe AER including uncorrectable/correctable status, masks, and severity selection. First Error Pointer and header logs capture failing TLP headers. Root-side registers report received error classes and can raise SERR/PME as configured. Software should classify severity and mask/report per system policy.
dependencies: Enable DEV_STAT_CTRL error reporting and ROOT_ERR_CMD; Configure masks/severity
Remarks: Disable ECRC if link partner doesn’t support it; use header logs to triage and correlate to transactions.

feature_name: Power management: D-states and ASPM L0s/L1
description: Device power states D0/D1/D2/D3hot/cold are supported alongside link power states L0s/L1. PMCAP/PM_CTL_STAT configure PME capabilities and device state transitions. LINK_STAT_CTRL controls ASPM entries; ACK_FREQ provides timers for L0s/L1 and NFTS values. L2 link state is not supported; entry to L2/L3 Ready is gated by PMCFG.
dependencies: PMCAP/PM_CTL_STAT fields; LINK_STAT_CTRL.ACTIVE_LINK_PM; ACK_FREQ programming
Remarks: Coordinate device D-states with link ASPM; beware reordering wake vs. power controls, and lack of L2 support.

feature_name: Reset mechanisms: POR/System reset and Software Hot Reset
description: Hardware power-on and system resets are supported; software can issue a downstream hot reset using RSTCMD.INIT_RST. After any reset, a minimum 100 ms wait is required before PCIe transactions, with up to 1.5 s tolerance if no response. PMRST interrupt group reports link reset requests and PM messages.
dependencies: Ensure traffic quiesced (ACT_STATUS); Respect post-reset delays; Re-init link via CMD_STATUS.LTSSM_EN
Remarks: On hot reset requests, disable LTSSM and drain transactions before issuing local reset and re-training.

feature_name: PIPE and PHY loopback for validation
description: PIPE loopback supports master/slave roles for symbol-level tests, while PHY loopback returns TX data to RX at the PHY for TLP testing without a partner. Control uses PL_LINK_CTRL.LPBK_EN for PIPE and SERDES_CFGx RX/TX loopback for PHY, with PL_FORCE_LINK to set LTSSM state when needed. Additional steps ensure sequence number alignment and training.
dependencies: SerDes configured and PLL locked; Loopback bits set; Force link to POLL_ACTIVE for PHY loopback
Remarks: Configure loopback before traffic; avoid BAR0 address matches; skip configuration-type transactions in loopback.

feature_name: Clocking and SerDes PLL control
description: The PHY uses a quality differential reference clock to generate functional and bit clocks via an internal PLL. Software programs PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL (PLL multiply, bandwidth, sleep, VCO range) and waits for PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK before enabling LTSSM. Common-clock usage must be advertised for SSC systems.
dependencies: Stable refclk; PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL.ENPLL=1; PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK=1; LINK_STAT_CTRL.COMMON_CLK_CFG
Remarks: Select loop bandwidth to match refclk quality; allow regulator settle time prior to PLL lock checks.

feature_name: LTSSM state visibility and debug registers
description: DEBUG0/DEBUG1 expose live PIPE signals, training indicators, and the LTSSM state encoding. Appendix A maps encoded LTSSM values (e.g., 0x11=L0) to textual states for diagnostics. These help verify training progress, lane reversal, electrical idle, and skip ordered sets. They are read-only views for runtime monitoring.
dependencies: Accessible via Address Space 0 Application Registers; Link active for meaningful values
Remarks: Poll LTSSM for bring-up scripts; log state transitions during interoperability testing.

feature_name: Transaction and alignment requirements
description: Maximum outbound payload is 128 bytes and inbound payload is 256 bytes, with remote read request size up to 256 bytes. Writes must present a single contiguous byte-strobe run; misaligned outbound transactions beyond 120 bytes are unspecified. Only incremental bursts are supported; fixed or wrap bursts can lock the bus. Read interleaving is avoided if read size ≤ max transaction size.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; Section 2.10.1 (Bus Mastering, Alignment, Burst, Read Interleaving); TLPCFG (3.1.6)
dependencies: DEV_STAT_CTRL.MAX_PAYLD & MAX_REQ_SZ; TLPCFG.RELAXED/NO_SNOOP; Proper buffer alignment
Remarks: Design DMA descriptors to 128B boundaries; keep misaligned burst size ≤120B; avoid non-incremental bursts.

feature_name: Configuration access: local/remote and IO window (RC)
description: RC performs Type0/1 configuration via CFG_SETUP and a 4KB remote configuration window in Address Space 0. An IO access window (RC only) maps 4KB internal space to IO TLPs with base from IOBASE. Local config space is accessible at 0x1000 offset within Address Space 0. Software must avoid remote accesses when link is down.
evidence: Section 2.11.1.2; 2.8.1; CFG_SETUP (3.1.4); IOBASE (3.1.5); Config common/type regs (3.2–3.4)
dependencies: Program CFG_SETUP (bus/dev/fn/type); IOBASE[31:12]; Link up
Remarks: Use 32-bit single accesses; gate RC remote config and IO windows on link status.

feature_name: ACK/Replay timing and filter controls
description: Port Logic offers programmable ACK frequency, NFTS for L0s exit, and latency timers for ACK/Nak and Replay. Additional symbol/timer knobs (SYM_NUM, SYMTIMER_FLTMASK) and FC watchdog controls aid tuning and robustness. These should be left at defaults unless interoperability requires adjustment.
dependencies: Stable link; Understanding of partner timing; Test coverage for modified values
Remarks: Change timers conservatively; validate under SSC and worst-case channel conditions.

feature_name: Security features
description: The specification does not describe explicit security mechanisms such as access control, authentication, or on-link encryption. Error reporting and protocol robustness are provided but are not security primitives. BAR0 always exposes application registers to RC by design. System-level isolation and secure boot are out of scope of this module. Treat security policy as a platform responsibility.
dependencies: UNKNOWN
Remarks: Harden access at system interconnect level; restrict BAR mappings and use MPU/IOMMU where available.
